revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 岐阜県関市貴船町20番地 |位置 = |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高靇大神]] [[美都波賣之神]] |社格 = 金弊社 |創建 = 天平18年(伝) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日 |神事 = }}
'''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]。
== 概要 == [[貴船神社]]は水の神、[[摂美都波賣之神]]、[[高靇大神]]を祭り、旧稱八龍社又は八龍大菩薩と言う。
その昔八龍社には雲龍山一透寺と言う寺院があったが、[[明治三年]][[十二月]]神仏分離により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる。
里伝に[[天平]]18年6月勧請する社と有り、美濃国神明帳によれば、正六位上梧山明神とあり、又異本神明帳によれば、正五位下桐山明神ともあり、いずれも当社を指すものと考えられる。
[[神社]]）は古来より祈雨乞雨の神として朝廷の崇敬特に篤く、史実にも明らかであり、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として一般庶民又水の関係者の信仰特に篤く、諸願成就の霊験顕著な神として崇めらる。
明治四年三月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって神饌幣帛料を供進する神社に指定され、村社より郷社となり、[[昭和]]21年4月[[岐阜県]]神社の金幣社となる。
祭典は4月第三土・日曜日に献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞神楽を奉納する。
== 祭神 == *[[摂美都波賣之神]] *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやせきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:関市]]

'''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]。
== 概要 == [[貴船神社]]は水の神、[[摂美都波賣之神]]、[[高靇大神]]を祭り、旧稱八龍社又は八龍大菩薩と言う。
その昔八龍社には雲龍山一透寺と言う寺院があったが、[[明治]]三年[[十二月]]神仏分離により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる。
里伝に[[天平]]18年6月勧請する社と有り、美濃国[[神明帳]]によれば、正六位上梧山明神とあり、又異本神明帳によれば、正五位下桐山明神ともあり、いずれも当社を指すものと考えられる。
[[神社]]は古来より祈雨乞雨の神として朝廷の崇敬特に篤く、史実にも明らかであり、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として一般庶民又水の関係者の信仰特に篤く、諸願成就の霊験顕著な神として崇めらる。
明治四年三月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって神饌幣帛料を供進する神社に指定され、[[村社]]より[[郷社]]となり、[[昭和]]21年4月[[岐阜県]]神社の[[金幣社]]となる。
祭典は4月第三土・日曜日に献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞神楽を奉納する。

[[神社]]は古来より祈雨乞雨の神として朝廷の崇敬特に篤く、史実にも明らかであり、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として一般庶民又水の関係者の信仰特に篤く、諸願成就の霊験顕著な神として崇めらる。

revision.comment: 誤字及び正字を改め、体裁を補填してみる。「要出典」が多いですね
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 岐阜県関市貴船町20 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高靇大神]]
[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]{{要出典|「美都波'''賣之'''」は「美都波'''之売（賣）'''」ではないのか？また、下述の順序や「摂」の字の有無に相異があるのは何故？|date=2009年2月}} |社格 = 旧郷社・金幣社 |創建 = 伝天平18年(746年) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日{{要出典|下記には「祭典は4月第三土・日曜日」とあるが？|date=2009年2月}} |神事 = }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[貴船神社]]は水の神、[[ミツハノメ|摂美都波賣之神]]{{要出典|テンプレート欄には「摂」の字がないが？|date=2009年2月}}、[[淤加美神|高靇大神]]を祭り、旧称「八龍社」または「八龍大菩薩」と言う。 その昔八龍社には雲龍山一透寺と言う寺院があったが、[[明治]]3年12月[[神仏分離]]により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる。 里伝に[[天平]]18年([[746年]])6月勧請する社と有り、『[[国内神名帳|美濃国神名記]]』によれば、「正六位上梧山明神」とあり、又異本神明帳によれば、「正五位下桐山明神」ともあり、いずれも当社を指すものと考えられる。 当神社は古来より[[雨乞い|祈雨]]の神として朝廷の崇敬特に篤く、史実にも明らかであり、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として一般庶民又水の関係者の信仰特に篤く、諸願成就の霊験顕著な神として崇めらる。 明治4年3月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって[[神饌幣帛料]]を供進する神社に指定され{{要出典|府県社以下に対する「神饌幣帛料供進」は明治39年の勅令第96号による規定からでは？|date=2009年2月}}、[[村社]]より[[郷社]]となり、[[昭和]]21年4月[[岐阜県]]神社の[[金幣社]]となる。 祭典は4月第3土・日曜日に献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞[[神楽]]を奉納する。 == 祭神 == *[[ミツハノメ|摂美都波賣之神]]{{要出典|上述|date=2009年2月}} *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやせきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:関市]]

{{告知|質問|御祭神・神饌幣帛料供進制度・その他について}} {{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 岐阜県関市貴船町20 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高靇大神]]
[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]{{要出典|「美都波'''賣之'''」は「美都波'''之売（賣）'''」ではないのか？また、下述の順序や「摂」の字の有無に相異があるのは何故？|date=2009年2月}} |社格 = 旧郷社・金幣社 |創建 = 伝天平18年(746年) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日 |神事 = }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[貴船神社]]は水の神、[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]{{要出典|テンプレート欄には「摂」の字がないが？|date=2009年2月}}、[[淤加美神|高靇大神]]を祭り、旧称「八龍社」または「八龍大菩薩」と言う。 その昔八龍社には雲龍山一透寺と言う寺院があったが、[[明治]]3年12月[[神仏分離]]により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる。 里伝に[[天平]]18年([[746年]])6月勧請する社と有り、『[[国内神名帳|美濃国神名記]]』によれば、「正六位上梧山明神」とあり、又異本神明帳{{要出典|神「明」帳でいいのか？|date=2009年2月}}によれば、「正五位下桐山明神」ともあり、いずれも当社を指すものと考えられる。 当神社は古来より[[雨乞い|祈雨]]の神として朝廷の崇敬特に篤く、史実にも明らかであり、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として一般庶民又水の関係者の信仰特に篤く、諸願成就の霊験顕著な神として崇めらる。 明治4年3月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって[[神饌幣帛料]]を供進する神社に指定され{{要出典|府県社以下に対する「神饌幣帛料供進」は明治39年の勅令第96号による規定からでは？|date=2009年2月}}、[[村社]]より[[郷社]]となり、[[昭和]]21年4月[[岐阜県]]神社の[[金幣社]]となる。 祭典は4月第3土・日曜日に献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞[[神楽]]を奉納する。 == 祭神 == *[[ミツハノメ|摂美都波賣之神]]{{要出典|上述|date=2009年2月}} *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] ==祭事== *元旦祭　1月1日 *天神祭　1月25日 *星祭大祓祭　2月3日 *節分大祈祷祭 *試楽祭　4月第3土曜日 *本楽祭　4月第3日曜日 *大祓祭　6月30日 *茅の輪くぐり神事　6月30日 *八龍社例祭　8月1日 *諏訪神社例祭　9月15日 *祖霊祭　9月23日 *明治節祭　11月3日 *秋祭感謝祭　11月23日 *天長節祭　12月23日 *大祓祭　12月31日 *除夜祭　12月31日 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやせきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:関市]]

{{告知|質問|神饌幣帛料供進制度・その他について}} {{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 岐阜県関市貴船町20 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]
[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] |社格 = 旧郷社・金幣社 |創建 = 伝天平18年(746年) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日 |神事 = }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[貴船神社]]は水の神、[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]、[[淤加美神|高靇大神]]を祭り、旧称「八龍社」または「八龍大菩薩」と言う。 その昔八龍社には雲龍山一透寺と言う寺院があったが、[[明治]]3年12月[[神仏分離]]により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる。 里伝に[[天平]]18年([[746年]])6月勧請する社と有り、『[[国内神名帳|美濃国神名記]]』によれば、「正六位上梧山明神」とあり、又異本神明帳{{要出典|神「明」帳でいいのか？|date=2009年2月}}によれば、「正五位下桐山明神」ともあり、いずれも当社を指すものと考えられる。 当神社は古来より[[雨乞い|祈雨]]の神として朝廷の崇敬特に篤く、史実にも明らかであり、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として一般庶民又水の関係者の信仰特に篤く、諸願成就の霊験顕著な神として崇めらる。 明治4年3月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって[[神饌幣帛料]]を供進する神社に指定され{{要出典|府県社以下に対する「神饌幣帛料供進」は明治39年の勅令第96号による規定からでは？|date=2009年2月}}、[[村社]]より[[郷社]]となり、[[昭和]]21年4月[[岐阜県]]神社の[[金幣社]]となる。 祭典は4月第3土・日曜日に献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞[[神楽]]を奉納する。 == 祭神 == *[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]] *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] ==祭事== *元旦祭　1月1日 *天神祭　1月25日 *星祭大祓祭　2月3日 *節分大祈祷祭 *試楽祭　4月第3土曜日 *本楽祭　4月第3日曜日 *大祓祭　6月30日 *茅の輪くぐり神事　6月30日 *八龍社例祭　8月1日 *諏訪神社例祭　9月15日 *祖霊祭　9月23日 *明治節祭　11月3日 *秋祭感謝祭　11月23日 *天長節祭　12月23日 *大祓祭　12月31日 *除夜祭　12月31日 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやせきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:関市]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 岐阜県関市貴船町20 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]
[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] |社格 = 旧郷社・金幣社 |創建 = 伝天平18年(746年) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日 |神事 = }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[貴船神社]]は水の神、[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]、[[淤加美神|高靇大神]]を祭り、旧称「八龍社」または「八龍大菩薩」と言う。 == 祭神 == *[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]] *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] == 由緒 == その昔八龍社には雲龍山一透寺と言う寺院があったが、[[明治]]3年12月[[神仏分離]]により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる。 里伝に[[天平]]18年([[746年]])6月勧請する社と有り、『[[国内神名帳|美濃国神名記]]』によれば、「正六位上梧山明神」とあり、又異本神明帳{{要出典|title=神「明」帳でいいのか？|date=2009年2月}}によれば、「正五位下桐山明神」ともあり、いずれも当社を指すものと考えられる。 当神社は古来より[[雨乞い|祈雨]]の神として朝廷の崇敬特に篤く、史実にも明らかであり、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として一般庶民又水の関係者の信仰特に篤く、諸願成就の霊験顕著な神として崇められる。 明治4年3月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって[[神饌幣帛料]]を供進する神社に指定され{{要出典|title=府県社以下に対する「神饌幣帛料供進」は明治39年の勅令第96号による規定からでは？|date=2009年2月}}、[[村社]]より[[郷社]]となり、[[昭和]]21年4月[[岐阜県]]神社の[[金幣社]]となる。 祭典は4月第3土・日曜日に献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞[[神楽]]を奉納する。 ==祭事== *元旦祭　1月1日 *天神祭　1月25日 *星祭大祓祭　2月3日 *節分大祈祷祭 *試楽祭　4月第3土曜日 *本楽祭　4月第3日曜日 *大祓祭　6月30日 *茅の輪くぐり神事　6月30日 *八龍社例祭　8月1日 *諏訪神社例祭　9月15日 *祖霊祭　9月23日 *明治節祭　11月3日 *秋祭感謝祭　11月23日 *天長節祭　12月23日 *大祓祭　12月31日 *除夜祭　12月31日 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやせきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社|岐阜]] [[Category:関市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 岐阜県関市貴船町20 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]

[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] |社格 = 旧郷社・金幣社 |創建 = 伝天平18年(746年) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日 |神事 = }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[貴船神社]]は水の神、[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]、[[淤加美神|高靇大神]]を祭り、旧称「八龍社」または「八龍大菩薩」と言う。 == 祭神 == *[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]] *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] == 由緒 == その昔八龍社には雲龍山一透寺と言う寺院があったが、[[明治]]3年12月[[神仏分離]]により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる。 里伝に[[天平]]18年([[746年]])6月勧請する社と有り、『[[国内神名帳|美濃国神名記]]』によれば、「正六位上梧山明神」とあり、又異本神明帳{{要出典|title=神「明」帳でいいのか？|date=2009年2月}}によれば、「正五位下桐山明神」ともあり、いずれも当社を指すものと考えられる。 当神社は古来より[[雨乞い|祈雨]]の神として朝廷の崇敬特に篤く、史実にも明らかであり、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として一般庶民又水の関係者の信仰特に篤く、諸願成就の霊験顕著な神として崇められる。 明治4年3月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって[[神饌幣帛料]]を供進する神社に指定され{{要出典|title=府県社以下に対する「神饌幣帛料供進」は明治39年の勅令第96号による規定からでは？|date=2009年2月}}、[[村社]]より[[郷社]]となり、[[昭和]]21年4月[[岐阜県]]神社の[[金幣社]]となる。 祭典は4月第3土・日曜日に献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞[[神楽]]を奉納する。 ==祭事== *元旦祭　1月1日 *天神祭　1月25日 *星祭大祓祭　2月3日 *節分大祈祷祭 *試楽祭　4月第3土曜日 *本楽祭　4月第3日曜日 *大祓祭　6月30日 *茅の輪くぐり神事　6月30日 *八龍社例祭　8月1日 *諏訪神社例祭　9月15日 *祖霊祭　9月23日 *明治節祭　11月3日 *秋祭感謝祭　11月23日 *天長節祭　12月23日 *大祓祭　12月31日 *除夜祭　12月31日 == 文化財 == *県指定文化財 **太刀{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=太刀 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} *市指定文化財 **安堵、寄進状{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=指定文化財 |publisher=関市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} **喜田吉右衛門書 **紫山伊兵衛書 **脇差 **石造灯籠 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやせきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社|岐阜]] [[Category:関市]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = [[File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町20 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]
[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] |社格 = 旧郷社・[[金幣社]] |創建 = 伝天平18年(746年) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日 |神事 = }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[貴船神社]]は水の神、[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]、[[淤加美神|高靇大神]]を祭り、旧称「八龍社」または「八龍大菩薩」と言う。 == 祭神 == *[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]] *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] == 由緒 == その昔八龍社には雲龍山一透寺と言う寺院があったが、[[明治]]3年12月[[神仏分離]]により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる。 里伝に[[天平]]18年([[746年]])6月勧請する社と有り、『[[国内神名帳|美濃国神名記]]』によれば、「正六位上梧山明神」とあり、又異本神明帳{{要出典|title=神「明」帳でいいのか？|date=2009年2月}}によれば、「正五位下桐山明神」ともあり、いずれも当社を指すものと考えられる。 当神社は古来より[[雨乞い|祈雨]]の神として朝廷の崇敬特に篤く、史実にも明らかであり、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として一般庶民又水の関係者の信仰特に篤く、諸願成就の霊験顕著な神として崇められる。 明治4年3月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって[[神饌幣帛料]]を供進する神社に指定され{{要出典|title=府県社以下に対する「神饌幣帛料供進」は明治39年の勅令第96号による規定からでは？|date=2009年2月}}、[[村社]]より[[郷社]]となり、[[昭和]]21年4月[[岐阜県]]神社の[[金幣社]]となる。 祭典は4月第3土・日曜日に献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞[[神楽]]を奉納する。 ==祭事== *元旦祭　1月1日 *天神祭　1月25日 *星祭大祓祭　2月3日 *節分大祈祷祭 *試楽祭　4月第3土曜日 *本楽祭　4月第3日曜日 *大祓祭　6月30日 *茅の輪くぐり神事　6月30日 *八龍社例祭　8月1日 *諏訪神社例祭　9月15日 *祖霊祭　9月23日 *明治節祭　11月3日 *秋祭感謝祭　11月23日 *天長節祭　12月23日 *大祓祭　12月31日 *除夜祭　12月31日 == 文化財 == *県指定重要文化財 **太刀{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=太刀 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} *市指定文化財 **安堵、寄進状{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=指定文化財 |publisher=関市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} **喜田吉右衛門書 **紫山伊兵衛書 **脇差 **石造灯籠 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや せきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:関市の歴史]] [[Category:関市の建築物]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]]

{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町20 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]
[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] |社格 = 旧郷社、[[金幣社]] |創建 = 伝：天平18年（746年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日 |神事 = }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]（[[貴船神社]]）。 水の神である[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]などを祀っている。旧称は「八龍社」や「八龍大菩薩」。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]（みずはのめのかみ） ; 摂末社祭神 * [[淤加美神|高靇大神]]（たかおかみのかみ） * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]（たけみなかたのかみ） * [[菅原道真]]（すがわらみちざね） == 歴史 == === 伝承 === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|境内]] 里伝によると、[[天平]]18年（746年）6月に勧請した社とある。『[[国内神名帳|美濃国神名記]]』による「正六位上梧山明神」、又異本神明帳{{要出典|title=神「明」帳でいいのか？|date=2009年2月}}による「正五位下桐山明神」は当社を指すものと考えられる。 古来より[[雨乞い|祈雨]]の神として朝廷の崇敬が篤く、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として庶民や水の関係者の信仰が篤い。 === 近現代 === その昔、八龍社には雲龍山一透寺と言う寺院があったが、1870年（明治3年）12月には[[神仏分離]]により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる。 1871年（明治4年）3月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって[[神饌幣帛料]]を供進する神社に指定され{{要出典|title=府県社以下に対する「神饌幣帛料供進」は明治39年の勅令第96号による規定からでは？|date=2009年2月}}、村社から郷社に昇格した。戦後に[[近代社格制度]]が廃止されると、1946年（昭和21年）4月には岐阜県神社庁によって[[金幣社]]に指定された。 == 祭事 == 毎年4月第3土曜・日曜、献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞[[神楽]]を奉納する。 *元旦祭　1月1日 *天神祭　1月25日 *星祭大祓祭　2月3日 *節分大祈祷祭 *試楽祭　4月第3土曜日 *本楽祭　4月第3日曜日 *大祓祭　6月30日 *茅の輪くぐり神事　6月30日 *八龍社例祭　8月1日 *諏訪神社例祭　9月15日 *祖霊祭　9月23日 *明治節祭　11月3日 *秋祭感謝祭　11月23日 *天長節祭　12月23日 *大祓祭　12月31日 *除夜祭　12月31日 == 文化財 == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|石造灯籠（関市指定文化財）]] === 岐阜県指定文化財 === * 太刀{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=太刀 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 関市指定文化財 === * 安堵、寄進状{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=指定文化財 |publisher=関市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * 喜田吉右衛門書 * 紫山伊兵衛書 * 脇差 * 石造灯籠 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 貴船神社] 岐阜県神社庁 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:関市の歴史]] [[Category:関市の建築物]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]]

{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町20 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]
[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] |社格 = 旧郷社、[[金幣社]] |創建 = 伝：天平18年（746年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日 |神事 = }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]（[[貴船神社]]）。 水の神である[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]などを祀っている。旧称は「八龍社」や「八龍大菩薩」。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]（みずはのめのかみ） ; 摂末社祭神 * [[淤加美神|高靇大神]]（たかおかみのかみ） * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]（たけみなかたのかみ） * [[菅原道真]]（すがわらみちざね） == 歴史 == === 伝承 === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|境内]] 里伝によると、[[天平]]18年（746年）6月に勧請した社とある。『[[国内神名帳|美濃国神名記]]』による「正六位上梧山明神」、又異本神明帳{{要出典|title=神「明」帳でいいのか？|date=2009年2月}}による「正五位下桐山明神」は当社を指すものと考えられる。 古来より[[雨乞い|祈雨]]の神として朝廷の崇敬が篤く、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として庶民や水の関係者の信仰が篤い。 === 近現代 === その昔、八龍社には[[別当寺]]として[[新長谷寺]]の末寺の[[真言宗]]雲龍山一透寺があったが、1870年（明治3年）12月には[[神仏分離]]により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる岐阜県文化財保護センター『岐阜県古代・中世寺院跡総合調査報告書 第三分冊 (中濃圏域)』 2023年 p.10。 1871年（明治4年）3月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって[[神饌幣帛料]]を供進する神社に指定され{{要出典|title=府県社以下に対する「神饌幣帛料供進」は明治39年の勅令第96号による規定からでは？|date=2009年2月}}、村社から郷社に昇格した。戦後に[[近代社格制度]]が廃止されると、1946年（昭和21年）4月には岐阜県神社庁によって[[金幣社]]に指定された。 == 祭事 == 毎年4月第3土曜・日曜、献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞[[神楽]]を奉納する。 *元旦祭　1月1日 *天神祭　1月25日 *星祭大祓祭　2月3日 *節分大祈祷祭 *試楽祭　4月第3土曜日 *本楽祭　4月第3日曜日 *大祓祭　6月30日 *茅の輪くぐり神事　6月30日 *八龍社例祭　8月1日 *諏訪神社例祭　9月15日 *祖霊祭　9月23日 *明治節祭　11月3日 *秋祭感謝祭　11月23日 *天長節祭　12月23日 *大祓祭　12月31日 *除夜祭　12月31日 == 文化財 == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|石造灯籠（関市指定文化財）]] === 岐阜県指定文化財 === * 太刀{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=太刀 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 関市指定文化財 === * 安堵、寄進状{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=指定文化財 |publisher=関市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * 喜田吉右衛門書 * 紫山伊兵衛書 * 脇差 * 石造灯籠 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 貴船神社] 岐阜県神社庁 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:関市の歴史]] [[Category:関市の建築物]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]]

revision.comment: removing the bad history import for new history imports
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|12=draft}} Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|12=draft}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|12=draft}} in Kifune Town. It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=draft}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu". == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=draft}} (mizuhanome no kami) ; Auxiliary Deities * {{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|12=draft}} (takaokami no kami) * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-kami|ja|タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata-no-kami|lt_ja=建御名方神|12=draft}} (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} (sugawara no michizane) == History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]] According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|12=draft}} 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=draft}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine. Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ({{ill|Amagoi|ja|雨乞い|lt=Prayer for Rain|lt_ja=祈雨|12=draft}}), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water. === Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine. In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=draft}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|12=draft}} was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency. == Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}}—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine}} [[ja:貴船神社_(関市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Seki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki City]] [[Category:Former Community Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages Goodbye $1]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:Untranslated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Seki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|12=draft}} Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|12=draft}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|12=draft}} in Kifune Town. It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=draft}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu". == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=draft}} (mizuhanome no kami) ; Auxiliary Deities * {{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|12=draft}} (takaokami no kami) * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-kami|ja|タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata-no-kami|lt_ja=建御名方神|12=draft}} (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} (sugawara no michizane) == History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]] According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|12=draft}} 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=draft}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine. Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ({{ill|Amagoi|ja|雨乞い|lt=Prayer for Rain|lt_ja=祈雨|12=draft}}), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water. === Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine. In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=draft}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|12=draft}} was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency. == Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}}—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine}} [[ja:貴船神社_(関市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Seki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki City]] [[Category:Former Community Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages Goodbye $1]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:Untranslated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages to be overwritten]] }} [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Seki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町20 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]
[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] |社格 = 旧郷社、[[金幣社]] |創建 = 伝：天平18年（746年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月1日 |神事 = }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]貴船町にある[[神社]]（[[貴船神社]]）。 水の神である[[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]などを祀っている。旧称は「八龍社」や「八龍大菩薩」。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[ミツハノメ|美都波賣之神]]（みずはのめのかみ） ; 摂末社祭神 * [[淤加美神|高靇大神]]（たかおかみのかみ） * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]（たけみなかたのかみ） * [[菅原道真]]（すがわらみちざね） == 歴史 == === 伝承 === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|境内]] 里伝によると、[[天平]]18年（746年）6月に勧請した社とある。『[[国内神名帳|美濃国神名記]]』による「正六位上梧山明神」、又異本神明帳{{要出典|title=神「明」帳でいいのか？|date=2009年2月}}による「正五位下桐山明神」は当社を指すものと考えられる。 古来より[[雨乞い|祈雨]]の神として朝廷の崇敬が篤く、神社資料にも祈雨祭は風千霖毎に行うとある。慈雨の神として庶民や水の関係者の信仰が篤い。 === 近現代 === その昔、八龍社には[[別当寺]]として[[新長谷寺]]の末寺の[[真言宗]]雲龍山一透寺があったが、1870年（明治3年）12月には[[神仏分離]]により一透寺は廃寺となり、八龍社は改号して貴船神社となる岐阜県文化財保護センター『岐阜県古代・中世寺院跡総合調査報告書 第三分冊 (中濃圏域)』 2023年 p.10。 1871年（明治4年）3月、岐阜県告示第八十号をもって[[神饌幣帛料]]を供進する神社に指定され{{要出典|title=府県社以下に対する「神饌幣帛料供進」は明治39年の勅令第96号による規定からでは？|date=2009年2月}}、村社から郷社に昇格した。戦後に[[近代社格制度]]が廃止されると、1946年（昭和21年）4月には岐阜県神社庁によって[[金幣社]]に指定された。 == 祭事 == 毎年4月第3土曜・日曜、献幣使を迎えて祭典を行い浦安舞[[神楽]]を奉納する。 *元旦祭　1月1日 *天神祭　1月25日 *星祭大祓祭　2月3日 *節分大祈祷祭 *試楽祭　4月第3土曜日 *本楽祭　4月第3日曜日 *大祓祭　6月30日 *茅の輪くぐり神事　6月30日 *八龍社例祭　8月1日 *諏訪神社例祭　9月15日 *祖霊祭　9月23日 *明治節祭　11月3日 *秋祭感謝祭　11月23日 *天長節祭　12月23日 *大祓祭　12月31日 *除夜祭　12月31日 == 文化財 == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|石造灯籠（関市指定文化財）]] === 岐阜県指定文化財 === * 太刀{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=太刀 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 関市指定文化財 === * 安堵、寄進状{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=指定文化財 |publisher=関市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * 喜田吉右衛門書 * 紫山伊兵衛書 * 脇差 * 石造灯籠 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 貴船神社] 岐阜県神社庁 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:関市の歴史]] [[Category:関市の建築物]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[貴船神社 (関市)]] to [[Kifune Shrine (Seki City)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Shrine |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|12=draft}} Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|12=draft}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|12=draft}} in Kifune Town. It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=draft}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu". == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=draft}} (mizuhanome no kami) ; Auxiliary Deities * {{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|12=draft}} (takaokami no kami) * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-kami|ja|タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata-no-kami|lt_ja=建御名方神|12=draft}} (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} (sugawara no michizane) == History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]] According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|12=draft}} 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=draft}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine. Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ({{ill|Amagoi|ja|雨乞い|lt=Prayer for Rain|lt_ja=祈雨|12=draft}}), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water. === Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine. In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=draft}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|12=draft}} was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency. == Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}}—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine}} [[ja:貴船神社_(関市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Seki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki City]] [[Category:Former Community Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages Goodbye $1]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages to be overwritten]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] }} [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Seki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (関市)|version=104505205|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft}} |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|qq=draft}} Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|qq=draft}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft}} |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|qq=draft}} in Kifune Town. It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu". == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft}} (mizuhanome no kami) ; Auxiliary Deities * {{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|qq=draft}} (takaokami no kami) * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-kami|ja|タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata-no-kami|lt_ja=建御名方神|qq=draft}} (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft}} (sugawara no michizane) == History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]] According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft}} 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine. Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ({{ill|Amagoi|ja|雨乞い|lt=Prayer for Rain|lt_ja=祈雨|qq=draft}}), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water. === Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}} led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine. In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency. == Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|qq=draft}}—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine}} [[ja:貴船神社_(関市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Seki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki City]] [[Category:Former Community Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages Goodbye $1]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages to be overwritten]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] }} [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Seki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (関市)|version=104505205|comment=Imported full ja history}}

the Urayasu Dance—a form of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|qq=draft}}—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki City|qq=}} Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=}}
{{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaokami-no-kami|qq=}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki City|qq=}} in Kifune Town. It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu". == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=}} (mizuhanome no kami) ; Auxiliary Deities * {{ill|Takaokami-no-kami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Takaokami-no-kami|lt_ja=高靇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaokami-no-kami|qq=}} (takaokami no kami) * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-kami|ja|タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata-no-kami|lt_ja=建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-kami|qq=}} (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} (sugawara no michizane) == History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]] According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō era|qq=}} 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|qq=}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine. Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ({{ill|Amagoi|ja|雨乞い|lt=Prayer for Rain|lt_ja=祈雨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagoi|qq=}}), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water. === Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine. In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency. == Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagura|qq=}}—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine}} [[ja:貴船神社_(関市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]][[Category:Kifune Shrines]][[Category:Seki, Gifu]][[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]][[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] {{Shrine |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=}}
[[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town. It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu". == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=}} (mizuhanome no kami) ; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} (sugawara no michizane) == History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]] According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|qq=}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine. Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water. === Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine. In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency. == Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine}} [[ja:貴船神社_(関市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]][[Category:Kifune Shrines]][[Category:Seki, Gifu]][[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu revision.comment: Bot: update category → History of Seki
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=}}
[[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town. It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu". == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=}} (mizuhanome no kami) ; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} (sugawara no michizane) == History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]] According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|qq=}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine. Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water. === Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine. In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency. == Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Shrine |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (1).jpg|280px]] |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}}
[[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town. It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu". == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}} (mizuhanome no kami) ; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sugawara no michizane) == History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]] According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|qq=}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine. Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water. === Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine. In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency. == Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency. == Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated. * New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31 == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]] === Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation {{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei * Wakizashi * Stone Lantern == References == '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|qq=}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|qid=Q8191796}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Zeki|qid=Q860647}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.48583|N|136.91194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|qq=}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>

== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
