{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|qid=Q8191796}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Zeki|qid=Q860647}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.48583|N|136.91194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|qq=|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|qid=Q8191796}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Zeki|qid=Q860647}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.48583|N|136.91194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|qq=|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>
== References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency

== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|qq=|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]

It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".

Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Zeki|WD=Q860647}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.48583|N|136.91194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Zeki|WD=Q860647}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.48583|N|136.91194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Zeki|WD=Q860647}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.48583|N|136.91194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>
== References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]

=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>

According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".

; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".

According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Zeki|WD=Q860647}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.48583|N|136.91194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Zeki|WD=Q860647}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.48583|N|136.91194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]] in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * [[:en:Kuraokami|Takaokami-no-kami]] (takaokami no kami) * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-kami]] (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ([[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Prayer for Rain]]), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Seki)]][[ja:貴船神社 (関市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634814]] Wikidata name: Kibune-jinja (Seki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kifune Shrine (貴船神社) |image = File:Kihune jinja seki.JPG |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} {{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki City}} Kifune Town 20 |location = {{coordinates|35|29|09|N|136|54|43|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |type = Former Community Shrine, {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} |founded = Tradition: Tenpyō 18 (746) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |festival = August 1 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Zeki|WD=Q860647}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.48583|N|136.91194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} {{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki City}} in Kifune Town.
It enshrines deities such as the water god {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}, and its former names include "Hachiryūsha" and "Hachiryū Daibosatsu".
== Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja|ミツハノメ|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-kami|lt_ja=美都波賣之神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhanome-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}} (mizuhanome no kami)
; Auxiliary Deities * {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami-no-kami}} (takaokami no kami) * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-kami}} (takeminakata no kami) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (sugawara no michizane)
== History == === Legend === [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki ac (2).jpg|thumb|View of the Shrine Grounds]]
According to local tradition, the shrine was enshrined in June of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era}} 18 (746). The "Seiroku-i jō Gozan Myōjin" as recorded in {{ill|Kokunishinmeichō|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Mino Province Shrine Register|lt_ja=美濃国神名記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokunishinmeichō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420490}}, and the "Seigo-i ge Tōyama Myōjin" as recorded in a variant Shimmeichō {{citation needed|title=Is "Shinmeiichō" acceptable?|date=February 2009}}, are considered to refer to this shrine.
Since ancient times, it has been revered by the imperial court as the deity of rain-making ({{ill|Rainmaking (ritual)|en|Rainmaking (ritual)|WD=Q477738|lt=Prayer for Rain}}), and according to shrine records, the rainmaking festival is held every Fūsenrin. As a benevolent rain deity, it has long enjoyed deep devotion among commoners and those connected with water.
=== Modern History === In the past, Hachiryūsha had a temple called Unryūzan Itto-ji, but in December 1870 (Meiji 3) the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}} led to the abolition of Itto-ji, and Hachiryūsha was renamed Kifune Shrine.
In March 1871 (Meiji 4), it was designated as a shrine to which offerings of sacred food and cloth ({{ill|Sacred Offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Offerings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were presented by Gifu Prefecture Notification No. 80 {{citation needed|title=Is the provision regarding offerings for shrines below the prefectural level derived from Meiji 39 Imperial Ordinance No. 96?|date=February 2009}}, and was elevated from a village shrine to a district shrine. After World War II, when the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was abolished, in April 1946 (Showa 21) it was designated as a {{ill|Kinheisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinheisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== Festivals == Every year on the third Saturday and Sunday of April, a festival is held with the presentation of offerings, and the Urayasu Dance—a form of {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}}—is dedicated.
* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
=== Seki City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Letter of Assurance and Donation <ref name="sekishi bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.seki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2865/16-14.doc |title=Cultural Property Designation |publisher=Seki City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * Calligraphy by Kida Kichibeimon <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Calligraphy by Murasaki Ihei <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Wakizashi <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/> * Stone Lantern <ref name="sekishi bunkazai"/>
== References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kibune-jinja (Seki)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=886 Kifune Shrine] Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency

* New Year's Festival – January 1 * Tenjin Festival – January 25 * Hoshi Matsuri Great Purification Festival – February 3 * Setsubun Great Prayer Festival * Trial Festival – Third Saturday of April * Main Festival – Third Sunday of April * Great Purification Festival – June 30 * Reed Circle Passing Ceremony – June 30 * Hachiryūsha Festival – August 1 * Suwa Shrine Festival – September 15 * Ancestor Spirit Festival – September 23 * Meiji Festival – November 3 * Autumn Festival Thanksgiving – November 23 * Tenchō Festival – December 23 * Great Purification Festival – December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival – December 31
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kibune-jinja Seki stone lantern ac (2).jpg|thumb|170px|Stone Lantern (Cultural Property designated by Seki City)]]
=== Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Sword <ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/sekisi/tachimei.html |title=Sword |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
